name: Voice RAG Chatbot with ElevenLabs and OpenAI
description: Automated workflow: Voice RAG Chatbot with ElevenLabs and OpenAI. This workflow integrates 15 different services: webhook, stickyNote, httpRequest, textSplitterTokenSplitter, vectorStoreQdrant. It contains 37 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
# STEP 4

## RAG System

Click on "test workflow" on n8n and "Test AI agent" on ElevenLabs. If everything is configured correctly, when you ask a question to the agent, the webhook on n8n is activated with the "question" field in the body filled with the question asked to the voice agent.

The AI ​​Agent will extract the information from the vector database, send it to the model to create the response which will be sent via the response webhook to ElevenLabs which will transform it into voice

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
# STEP 1

## Create an Agent on ElevenLabs 
- Create an agent on ElevenLabs (eg. test_n8n)
- Add "First message" (eg. Hi, Can I help you?)
- Add the "System Prompt" message... eg:
'You are the waiter of "Pizzeria da Michele" in Verona. If you are asked a question, use the tool "test_chatbot_elevenlabs". When you receive the answer from "test_chatbot_elevenlabs" answer the user clearly and precisely.'
- In Tools add a Webhook called eg. "test_chatbot_elevenlabs" and add the following description:
'You are the waiter. Answer the questions asked and store them in the question field.'
- Add the n8n webhook URL (method POST)
- Enable "Body Parameters" and insert in the description "Ask the user the question to ask the place.", then in the "Properties" add a data type string called "question", value type "LLM Prompt" and description "user question"

parameters.content:
# STEP 5

## Add Widget

Add the widget to your business website by replacing AGENT_ID with the agent id you created on ElevenLabs

<elevenlabs-convai agent-id="AGENT_ID"></elevenlabs-convai><script src="{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}" async type="text/javascript"></script>

parameters.content:
# STEP 1

## Create an Agent on ElevenLabs 
- Create an agent on ElevenLabs (eg. test_n8n)
- Add "First message" (eg. Hi, Can I help you?)
- Add the "System Prompt" message... eg:
'You are the waiter of "Pizzeria da Michele" in Verona. If you are asked a question, use the tool "test_chatbot_elevenlabs". When you receive the answer from "test_chatbot_elevenlabs" answer the user clearly and precisely.'
- In Tools add a Webhook called eg. "test_chatbot_elevenlabs" and add the following description:
'You are the waiter. Answer the questions asked and store them in the question field.'
- Add the n8n webhook URL (method POST)
- Enable "Body Parameters" and insert in the description "Ask the user the question to ask the place.", then in the "Properties" add a data type string called "question", value type "LLM Prompt" and description "user question"

# STEP 2

## Create Qdrant Collection
Change:
- QDRANTURL
- COLLECTION

# STEP 4

## RAG System

Click on "test workflow" on n8n and "Test AI agent" on ElevenLabs. If everything is configured correctly, when you ask a question to the agent, the webhook on n8n is activated with the "question" field in the body filled with the question asked to the voice agent.

The AI ​​Agent will extract the information from the vector database, send it to the model to create the response which will be sent via the response webhook to ElevenLabs which will transform it into voice

# STEP 3












## Documents vectorization with Qdrant and Google Drive
Change:
- QDRANTURL
- COLLECTION

# STEP 5

## Add Widget

Add the widget to your business website by replacing AGENT_ID with the agent id you created on ElevenLabs

<elevenlabs-convai agent-id="AGENT_ID"></elevenlabs-convai><script src="{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}" async type="text/javascript"></script>

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
google Drive  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Locate the webhook node named "Respond to ElevenLabs".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Locate the webhook node named "Listen".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Vector Store Tool" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Create collection" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Refresh collection" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get folder" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download Files" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Default Data Loader" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Qdrant Vector Store1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
